question: Describe the physical dimensions of the RPV.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a critical pressure boundary to prevent the release of fission products and provides structural support for various reactor components, with all materials and fabrication processes adhering to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a containment vessel. The RPV is a critical barrier to fission product release and is constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies the use of nondestructive examinations, controlled shipping environments, and quality assurance measures to ensure the integrity and safety of the reactor vessel. Key standards cited include the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code and ASME NQA-1 for quality assurance.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: Threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components use nickel-based Alloy 718, meeting ASME BPVC standards for material compatibility and stress corrosion cracking mitigation. Preservice and inservice inspections and testing of pressure-retaining components follow ASME BPVC Section XI and relevant regulatory requirements, with inspection programs updated to the latest approved code editions for each inspection interval.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, their protective cladding, and compliance with relevant BPVC material specifications to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, ensuring stable operation under various conditions.

edges.description: Section A and Section B both provide identical introductory descriptions of the RPV's function and context, but neither provides information about the physical dimensions, so they are semantically related by topic but do not answer the analysis question.

Section A provides a general description of the RPV's function and context, while Section B references standards and requirements for its shipment, installation, and inspection, connecting through the subject of the RPV but not directly addressing its physical dimensions.

Section A provides a general description of the RPV and its role in the reactor system, while Section B specifies material and design details for the RPV's threaded fasteners, thus providing detailed specifications for a component mentioned in Section A.

Section A describes the components and general arrangement of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B lists references related to the integrity and material standards of the reactor coolant boundary, which includes the RPV, thus Section A provides context for the physical system to which Section B's referenced standards apply.

Section B references Section A by citing the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report Reactor Vessel, indicating that the inspection and shipment requirements discussed in Section B pertain to the same RPV described in Section A.

Section A provides a general description of the RPV as a component of the reactor system, while Section B specifies the materials and standards for threaded fasteners used in the RPV's main closure flange, connecting the general vessel description to specific design details.

Section A references ASME NQA-1-2008/1a-2009 Addenda for RPV shipment and installation requirements, which is also cited in Section B as a quality assurance standard for nuclear facility applications, indicating both sections rely on the same regulatory standard.
Section A discusses the materials and standards for RPV threaded fasteners and cites Section B as a reference for applicable industry guidelines and standards.

Section A references specific components and materials of the RPV upper shell and head, while Section B provides a general description of the RPV's function and integration within the NuScale Power Module, connecting them through their discussion of RPV structure and context.

Section A references the materials and components of the RPV upper shell and head, while Section B provides a general description of the RPV's function and integration within the NuScale Power Module, connecting them through their discussion of the RPV's structure and materials.

Section A describes specific components and materials of the RPV upper shell and head, while Section B discusses the handling, shipment, and storage requirements for the entire RPV, connecting them through their focus on different aspects of the RPV's physical structure and integrity.

Section A describes the pressurizer as an integral part of the reactor vessel, while Section B provides a general description of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and its components, establishing that the pressurizer is physically contained within the RPV.

Section A describes the pressurizer as an integral part of the reactor vessel, while Section B provides a general description of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and its components, establishing that the pressurizer is physically contained within the RPV.

Section A describes the pressurizer as an integral part of the reactor vessel, establishing a physical relationship, while Section B discusses requirements for the reactor vessel itself; thus, Section A provides specific details about a component (pressurizer) that is part of the general system described in Section B.
